issues.title: Interactive curses launch is unguarded: a startup failure or event-loop exception exits 1 with a traceback (not the documented exit 2), so gogo-done misreads it as a user cancel and skips the mandated fallback
issues.description: assets/kanban/board.py:400 (run_board) calls `curses.wrapper(_board_loop, records)` with no try/except, and neither main() nor the `sys.exit(main())` entrypoint guards it. Any curses startup failure (bad/absent TERM, terminal that can't cbreak) OR any uncaught exception inside the event loop propagates: Python prints a full traceback and exits with code 1 and no intent file. Reproduced live: `python3 assets/kanban/board.py --index <idx> --result <out> </dev/null` -> multi-line traceback, exit=1, no result file; likewise with `env -u TERM`. This violates the file's OWN documented exit-code contract (docstring lines 30-37: "2 error -- ... or the board cannot start. No result file; a one-line reason on stderr and NO traceback"). Worse, the consuming skill relies on 1-vs-2 to tell a cancel from a crash: skills/gogo-done/SKILL.md:288 treats `no $res && $code == 1` as "board cancelled -- nothing shipped" and STOPS, while `exit 2` would route to the guaranteed status-table + AskUserQuestion fallback (lines 290-293). So a board that crashes on startup or mid-loop is silently reported to the user as a deliberate cancel and the fallback the NFR mandates (non-functional-requirements.md: "absence -> graceful fallback, never a failure") never fires. The reviewer brief explicitly called for exit codes 0/1/2 honored on every path including curses exceptions; this path is not honored. (The normal tmux launch usually gives curses a working pty, which is why selftest can't cover this and it slipped through, but a crash mid-loop is reachable in the real launch path.)
issues.proposed_solution:
AGENT-FIXABLE. Wrap the curses launch in run_board() so any failure maps to the documented exit 2 + one-line stderr + no traceback, e.g.:
    def run_board(records, result_path):
        import curses
        try:
            intent = curses.wrapper(_board_loop, records)
        except Exception as exc:  # curses.error and any event-loop crash
            print("board: cannot run the interactive board: %s" % exc, file=sys.stderr)
            return 2
        if intent is None:
            return 1
        emit_intent(result_path, intent)
        return 0
Ensure no partial result file is emitted on the error path (it already isn't, since emit_intent runs only after a clean return). curses.wrapper already restores the terminal (endwin) before the exception surfaces, so no extra teardown is needed. Optionally add a note that curses exceptions -> exit 2 is intentionally untestable via --selftest (no tty).
issues.fix_summary: run_board() now wraps curses.wrapper in try/except: any TUI startup/loop failure prints one stderr line ('board: cannot run the TUI: <exc>') and returns 2 -- a crash is an error, never a cancel. Verified live: TERM= + no tty -> exit 2, 1 stderr line, no traceback, no result file; --selftest still exit 0 (31/31).

issues.title: Stale validate-in wording in two FR5 reference docs contradicts the shipped relaxed cockpit gate (nothing-ready-to-ship now still opens the board)
issues.description: docs/commands.md:181 ("board mode with nothing ready-to-ship says so and stops without opening an empty board.") and docs/flow.md:107 ("board mode with nothing ready-to-ship says so instead of opening an empty board.") both describe the OLD gate. The feature relaxed validate-in (decisions.md implementation notes; skills/gogo-done/SKILL.md:76-82; commands/done.md:22-23): the cockpit now opens whenever ANY `.gogo/work/feature-*` exists so `v`/`g`/`/` work on non-shippable cards -- only ZERO features stops. README.md correctly dropped the old claim, but these two docs -- both explicitly listed for FR5 sync -- kept the stale line even though their surrounding cockpit prose was rewritten. This is exactly the all-of-docs/*.md doc-sync class code-review-standards.md elevates (the REV-001-in-0.8.0 lesson). A user/agent reading either reference would believe the cockpit refuses to open with nothing ready-to-ship, which the code and skill no longer do.
issues.proposed_solution: AGENT-FIXABLE. In both docs/commands.md:181 and docs/flow.md:107, replace the stale clause with the relaxed behavior, e.g.: "...; board mode with zero work items says so and stops without opening an empty board (with items present but none ready-to-ship the cockpit still opens for view/go/filter -- you just can't ship/merge until something is report-complete)." Match the wording already in skills/gogo-done/SKILL.md:76-82 and commands/done.md:22-23.
issues.fix_summary: docs/commands.md validate-in bullet + docs/flow.md board-mode line updated to the relaxed gate: the cockpit opens whenever any feature exists (v/g useful with nothing ready-to-ship); stops only at zero features. Now consistent with gogo-done SKILL, commands/done.md, README.
